given: VS4 contract and matrix are frozen.
then: Markdown and CSV exist, counts match 20/7/1, and every row has behavior, verification, evidence, pass/fail criteria, owner, and initial status.
verification: Source review, duplicate-ID check, row-count check, docs verifiers.
evidence: Contract path, matrix path, row-count output, scripts/verify_sot_docs.sh, git diff --check.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if contract and matrix are internally consistent and status-neutral; FAIL on duplicate IDs, missing criteria, count mismatch, or implementation PASS claim.

given: A user opens Product Alpha Home in local/dev mode.
then: Home shows Drop, Ask, Continue work, pending work, knowledge states, and recent activity without forcing admin or proof setup.
evidence: Browser screenshot or DOM, route/source refs, scenario report row.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if first view is product-first; FAIL if first value requires connector, ontology, model, policy, or scenario setup.

given: A user has messy input.
when: User drops, uploads, or pastes it.
then: Original input is preserved as Source / Artifact and UI confirms saved/searchable or partial/degraded status.
evidence: Artifact/source record, checksum/provenance, UI proof, audit refs.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if original is preserved before generated output; FAIL if generated summary hides missing original preservation.

given: Dropped or pasted input completes first processing.
when: CornerStone creates or prepares first work item.
then: An Evidence-backed Brief is created or prepared as the central work item.
evidence: Brief record/state, source refs, evidence refs, audit refs.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if Brief is a visible central product object; FAIL if user only sees raw search/proof output.

given: A user opens the Brief.
then: Brief shows summary, supported findings, gaps/uncertainty, claim candidates, memory candidates, suggested actions, evidence, and activity.
verification: Browser observation plus product object inspection.
evidence: Screenshot/DOM, Brief JSON/state, evidence linkage.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if supported findings and gaps are visible; FAIL on unsupported AI answer presented as fact.

given: A user sees evidence-linked finding in a Brief.
then: Evidence Drawer shows source, snippet, support rationale, provenance, related objects, activity, and audit detail.
verification: Browser observation plus evidence record inspection.
evidence: Evidence Drawer screenshot/DOM, evidence refs, provenance refs.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if evidence is one clear action away and resolves to source/provenance; FAIL if evidence is only decorative label.

given: A Brief contains a supported finding.
then: Claim candidate carries statement, trust state, rationale, supporting evidence, gaps, related Brief, and activity.
evidence: Claim record, evidence refs, trust state, audit refs.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if Claim candidate remains evidence-aware and scoped; FAIL if Claim loses evidence or trust state.

given: A Claim has no supporting evidence.
then: Approval is blocked or Claim remains Draft / Insufficient evidence with cause and resolution path.
verification: Automated negative test plus UI check.
evidence: Denial response, unchanged claim state, UI cause/resolution text, audit ref.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if zero-evidence approval creates no approved claim; FAIL if unsupported claim becomes authority.

given: A Brief or Claim suggests durable knowledge.
when: User creates or reviews Memory candidate.
then: Memory candidate is Draft / Needs Review with source, trust state, freshness, owner/workspace, and controls.
evidence: Memory candidate record, UI proof, source/evidence refs, audit refs.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if Memory candidate is visible and reviewable; FAIL if durable memory is hidden.

given: A Memory candidate exists before approval.
when: System considers memory for future use.
then: It is not hidden durable truth and cannot be used as Approved memory until reviewed or approved.
evidence: Approved-memory absence before approval, approved-memory presence only after approval, audit refs.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if pre-approval memory does not become approved truth; FAIL on silent memory write or hidden profiling.

given: A Brief or Claim suggests next action.
then: Action Card shows goal, why, supporting evidence, impacted objects, proposed change, expected impact, risk, safety check, approval state, execution mode, and activity.
verification: Browser observation plus action state inspection.
evidence: Action Card screenshot/DOM, action JSON/state, policy decision refs, audit refs.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if user can understand action before approval; FAIL if action appears as magic automation.

given: A user reviews Action Card execution mode in local/dev mode.
then: Execution mode is Draft / Local / Mock by default and no live external writeback is implied.
evidence: UI proof, action state, real_external_http_calls=0, audit refs.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if no live writeback is claimed or performed; FAIL on hidden or implied live external send.

given: A returning user has pending work.
then: Ops Inbox shows pending briefs, evidence gaps, claims, memory candidates, action cards, approvals, and recent activity with Continue links.
evidence: Fixture state, screenshot/DOM, route refs.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if the product supports returning daily work; FAIL if only one-shot demo flow exists.

given: User asks a question in AskBox.
then: Answer shows evidence/memory used and can become Brief, Claim, Memory candidate, or Action Card without becoming chatbot-only output.
evidence: Ask transcript, evidence/memory refs, created work item refs, audit refs.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if Ask can promote durable work with evidence; FAIL if it is generic chatbot output with no durable loop.

given: Three general-purpose fixture packs exist.
when: Each pack runs through VS4 UI loop.
then: Personal Research, Company Policy Review, and Operations Issue each produce Brief, Claim candidate, Memory candidate, Action Card draft, and Ops Inbox follow-up.
evidence: Fixture outputs, browser traces, scenario report rows.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if the same loop works across three non-logistics-only domains; FAIL if core requires logistics-specific concepts.

given: A user views or switches workspace context.
then: Active workspace/owner context is visible enough to understand which context CornerStone is using.
evidence: Workspace UI proof, JSON/state scope, zero cross-workspace ambiguity.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if context is visible and scoped; FAIL if user cannot tell which workspace informs results/actions.

given: A user scans normal-user VS4 UI.
when: Visible labels and copy are reviewed.
then: Product language appears before internal proof language; internal IDs/details are progressively disclosed.
evidence: Copy scan output, browser screenshots/DOM, allowed internal-detail locations.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if normal user can work without scenario/verifier/engine jargon; FAIL if internal proof language dominates.

given: Major VS4 pages are exercised across common states.
then: Empty, loading, ready, partial/degraded, needs-review, permission denied, policy blocked, failed-with-recovery, and audit/log available states are defined and observable or documented per page.
evidence: State coverage matrix, screenshots/DOM or component/source refs.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if required states are covered for major pages; FAIL if failure or blocked states are absent or misleading.

given: Home, Search, and Artifact surfaces are implemented or reviewed.
then: Surfaces follow reference direction for light calm shell, small nav, prominent search, original/source primacy, trust/evidence chips, and progressive evidence.
evidence: Screenshot/DOM, source refs, reference mapping notes; no PASS from images alone.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if implementation behavior and UI align with references; FAIL if images are treated as implementation evidence.

given: Claim and Action surfaces are implemented or reviewed.
then: Surfaces follow reference evidence/action patterns: trust ladder, evidence picker/drawer, dry-run, diff/impact, policy/risk, approval, execution mode, and activity/audit.
evidence: Screenshot/DOM, source refs, reference mapping notes; no PASS from images alone.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if implementation behavior and UI align with references; FAIL if images are treated as implementation evidence.

given: VS4 changes are applied.
then: Existing VS0 Artifact -> Search -> Evidence -> Claim -> Action -> Audit loop still passes on the same tree.
verification: Existing VS0 verifier and browser/API proof rerun.
evidence: Scenario report, CLI transcript, browser/API evidence.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if fresh VS0 proof passes; FAIL on stale report reuse or broken VS0 loop.

given: VS4 changes are applied over accepted VS1 ontology slice.
then: VS1 ontology suggest/review/promote still starts from evidence and keeps suggestions draft until explicit promotion.
verification: Existing VS1 verifier and browser/API proof rerun.
evidence: VS1 scenario report, zero auto-promotion evidence, audit refs.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if fresh VS1 proof passes; FAIL on forced modeling, auto-promotion, or evidence loss.

given: VS4 UI, reports, help, and README text are scanned.
when: Static overclaim scan and browser review run.
then: No production, on-prem, final security, real IdP, real network, live-provider, migration/restore, or human UX readiness claim appears unless explicitly historical/deferred.
verification: Static overclaim scan plus browser review.
evidence: Text scan output, browser observations, negative evidence counters.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if forbidden readiness claims are absent or historical/deferred; FAIL on unqualified readiness claim.

given: Prompt-injection artifact or Ask input attempts to approve memory/action or create authority.
then: No memory approval, claim approval, action execution, egress, policy change, or authority expansion occurs from untrusted content.
evidence: Zero unauthorized counters, blocked-attempt audit, untrusted label, evidence refs.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if untrusted content cannot create authority; FAIL on content-driven approval/action/egress.

given: VS4 verification report cites visual references.
when: Report lint or source review runs.
then: Reference images are treated as visual inputs only, not implementation PASS, scenario PASS, or human UX acceptance evidence.
evidence: Lint output or source refs showing images are design references only.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if image references are not used as PASS proof; FAIL if image existence marks a scenario passing.

given: Normal user opens VS4 after admin, connector, policy, or ontology work exists.
when: Browser/nav review and text scan run.
then: Home remains product-first and not admin/connector/ontology/scenario-verifier-first.
evidence: Screenshots/DOM, nav map, absence of admin-first route.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if default experience remains Drop / Ask / Continue; FAIL if admin or internal substrate becomes first value.

given: A VS4 feature appears in UI/API without CLI parity.
when: Scenario gate or parity review runs.
then: Scenario gate blocks PASS or marks feature NOT_VERIFIED until native CLI path and JSON transcript exist.
evidence: CLI transcript refs, missing-command failure row, scenario report status.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if missing CLI parity blocks feature PASS; FAIL if UI/API-only feature is accepted.

given: JiYong/Tars uses the VS4 Product Alpha UI flow.
then: Human accepts or rejects whether the daily loop is usable, understandable, product-first, evidence-aware, and not misleading.
evidence: Acceptance note with screenshots/recording, or rejection note with issue list.
pass_fail_criteria: Remains HUMAN_REQUIRED until JiYong/Tars records explicit acceptance; blocks product-alpha human UX acceptance claim.
